issues.description:
## Trigger
3-times rule hit: Vendor Brief + Changelog Verification pattern has run for Lovable, Bolt.new, and at least one other vendor in the Phase 6 sweep (DEV-62). Quality Gate Manager flagged this for extraction.

## Pattern to extract
A standardized workflow for producing a vendor competitive brief that includes:
1. Structured brief template (capabilities, what-they-beat-us-at, what-we-beat-them-at, key import patterns)
2. Changelog/feature announcement verification step (docs-analyst sub-task delegated to extract latest release notes)
3. Source citation requirement (minimum 3 sources)
4. Quality gate: correctness check against live docs, corrections documented, breaking changes noted

## Input
- Vendor name
- Primary website + changelog URL
- Phase / sweep context (e.g. Phase 6)

## Expected Output
- Skill definition in Multica skill format with: purpose, trigger, required input, steps, output, quality checklist, failure handling, improvement rule
- Prompt template for the docs-analyst sub-task

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skill spec written and reviewed
- [ ] Skill added to the Multica skill library
- [ ] benchmark-vendor-tracker updated to reference the new skill

## Definition of Done
Skill spec complete, registered in 08 - Skill Library, referenced from benchmark-vendor-tracker.
issues.title: SKILL: Vendor Brief + Changelog Verification — extract as reusable skill

issues.description:
Run the daily factory health audit. Cover: (1) runtime health (multica runtime list) — alert if any offline >5m; (2) WIP discipline — flag any agent with >3 in_progress or any squad with >6; (3) stale issues — list any in_progress issue >24h with no comments; (4) orphan/zero-skill check — every skill assigned to ≥1 agent; every agent has ≥1 skill; (5) metadata hygiene — sample 10 done issues and flag any with stale waiting_on / blocked_reason; (6) cost — pull the current month spend per project and compare to budget (cost-budget-tracker); (7) error budget — pull yesterday's Sentry error rate, latency p95, uptime % per shipped app and alert on SLO burn. Post one consolidated comment on the created issue with: GREEN/YELLOW/RED status, the top 5 issues found, and one-line remediation per issue. Status the issue 'done' if all green; 'in_review' if YELLOW; 'blocked' if RED. Do not silently ignore failures.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-05-27 03:00 UTC. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Factory Health Audit — 2026-05-27

issues.description:
## SKILL IMPORT: sandbox-runtime

**Source:** https://github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime
**License:** Apache 2.0 (green)
**Stars:** 4,122 | **Last commit:** 2026-05-23 (from Anthropic experimental)

### What it does
Lightweight OS-level sandboxing tool (`srt`) for restricting filesystem and network access on arbitrary agent processes without requiring containers.
- macOS: uses `sandbox-exec`
- Linux: uses `bubblewrap`
- Network: proxy-based filtering with allowlist
- No container runtime needed

### Proposed Multica skill name
`sandbox-runtime-policy`

### PR-Ready Spec
- **Install:** `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/sandbox-runtime`
- **Usage:** Wrap shell invocations: `srt "<command>"`
- **Integration:** Hook into `ci-cd-pipeline-builder` and `agentic-codegen-loop` skills — apply `srt` when agent executes shell commands during builds
- **Skill shape:** Define a SKILL.md that specifies which commands require sandboxing, what filesystem paths to allow, and which network endpoints to permit

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `srt` installs successfully in workspace Linux environment (bubblewrap path)
- [ ] At least one build command runs sandboxed without breaking normal operation
- [ ] Allowlist defaults documented for Supabase CLI, npm, gh CLI
- [ ] Security review sub-issue returns `decision=green`

### Dependencies
- Security review sub-issue (sibling, assigned to security-auditor) must be `done` + `decision=green` before this issue is promoted to `todo`

### Risk
- Sandbox itself needs audit — review allowlist defaults and privilege escalation paths
- Linux bubblewrap requires specific kernel capabilities; may need adjustments in Docker-based workspace

### Definition of Done
- Sandbox policy documented in `sandbox-runtime-policy` SKILL.md
- Integration hooked into at least one existing build skill
- Security review cleared
issues.title: SKILL IMPORT: sandbox-runtime

issues.description:
## Input
`dashboard-screen-planner` covers dashboards. There is no equivalent for general screens (auth, settings, onboarding, marketing, modal flows). Every UX-architect run rebuilds the structure by hand.

## Expected Output
Skill `ux-screen-generator`. Input: screen purpose (one sentence). Output: a structured spec:
- Primary CTA + secondary actions
- Data shown (fields + format)
- Permission gate (which role(s) can reach this)
- Empty / loading / error state variants (mandatory)
- Mobile breakpoint (≤ 375px) layout note
- RTL variant note (when target locale includes Arabic)
- Accessibility minimums (focus order, ARIA labels, contrast)
- Recommended components from the workspace design tokens

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output is implementation-ready (a frontend-engineer can build directly from it)
- [ ] Reused by `prd-to-user-stories` + `dashboard-screen-planner`
- [ ] Includes the 3 mandatory variants every time

## DoD
Skill published; product-manager + ux-architect agents have it in their skill list.
issues.title: SKILL: ux-screen-generator — general screen spec beyond dashboards (auth/settings/onboarding)

issues.description:
## Input
Parent: [DEV-25](mention://issue/f01f5264-86df-4f80-a2ff-f6a1abbb0794). PRD: [DEV-26](mention://issue/799e091f-184e-4551-8f8d-fd55cf62c59f). Architecture: [DEV-30](mention://issue/6d29ecb8-4487-4e92-a87b-cd672b72e219).

App Hub project: `09e6cfd3-d45e-4225-a494-2e3400b6983c`. Repo: `adminnewtech/attendance-app` (pending). Target lifecycle phase: `deploy_gate=green` cannot fire until QA + Security sign off on the `integrate` branch.

## Expected Output

A `DEPLOY.md` + `RUNBOOK.md` in the repo plus a comment on this issue summarising the plan. Cover:

1. **Environments**: dev (Supabase project + Vercel preview + EAS internal) → staging → prod (gated)
2. **Env vars list** for each environment (NO secret values — placeholder names only)
3. **Pre-deploy checklist**: migrations applied, RLS policies verified, FCM Vault secrets present, EAS credentials linked
4. **Deploy steps** (web admin: `vercel deploy --prod` after preview green; mobile: `eas build --profile production` + Play Store internal track first)
5. **Smoke tests** post-deploy (5 minute manual checklist: login, punch-in, punch-out, history, leave request, admin dashboard live-board)
6. **Rollback plan**: revert Vercel deployment in 1 click; mobile: pull from Play Store + previous APK rollout; DB: down-migrations for last 3 migrations
7. **Monitoring + alerting**: Sentry + Supabase logs + uptime check on edge functions + p95 latency budget
8. **Incident response runbook**: who to page, severity matrix, comms template (bilingual AR/EN)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DEPLOY.md` covers all 3 environments with env var names
- [ ] `RUNBOOK.md` covers smoke tests + rollback + monitoring + incident response
- [ ] Both documents are bilingual where customer-facing (incident comms template)
- [ ] No secret values written anywhere; only placeholder names
- [ ] Section "Pre-deploy gates" lists the `deploy_gate=green` requirements explicitly

## Dependencies
None blocking — Architecture is finalised. Can start immediately.

## Risk
- EAS production builds require paid Expo account — flag in docs as a paid resource gate.
- Vercel prod deploy requires custom domain DNS for `attendance.newtechkw.com` — document the DNS records needed.

## Definition of Done
Both files committed to `adminnewtech/attendance-app` (or local-pending). Comment on this issue with summary + PR/commit link. `deploy_gate=ready_to_arm` pinned on metadata once both QA + Security sign off lands.

metadata.blocked_reason: repo adminnewtech/attendance-app not provisioned in workspace — cannot commit artifacts to integrate branch until repo is configured

issues.title: Deployment Plan + Runbook — Android Attendance App
